title: Important Update: SEBI Revises SGF Coverage for Commodity Derivatives Segment
content:
# Important Update: SEBI Revises SGF Coverage for Commodity Derivatives Segment

Ek important update hai for all financial professionals! The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) ne commodity derivatives segment ke liye Settlement Guarantee Fund (SGF) ke coverage norms review kiye hain. This official update, issued on March 16, 2026, is particularly relevant for Chartered Accountants and Company Secretaries operating in the Indian financial markets.

Here's what you need to know:

*   **Circular Number & Date:** SEBI Circular No. HO/47/16/14(1)2026-MRD-POD1/1/7115/2026 dated March 16, 2026.
*   **Previous Norms:** Earlier, clearing corporations ko Core SGF ki coverage calculate karni hoti thi by considering the simultaneous default of at least two clearing members (and their associates) causing the highest credit exposure, along with 50% of the credit exposure from the simultaneous default of all clearing members.
*   **Revised Norms:** Under the new framework, clearing corporations will now calculate the credit exposure by assuming the simultaneous default of at least three clearing members (and their associates) that generate the highest credit exposure in stress-testing scenarios.
*   **Flexibility Provision:** SEBI ne ek naya provision bhi add kiya hai, jiske तहत regulator case-by-case basis par SGF norms ke strict enforcement se exemptions ya relaxations de sakta hai. This will depend on prevailing market conditions, the adequacy of risk management frameworks, and the broader objective of investor protection.
*   **Objective:** These changes aim to facilitate the 'Ease of Doing Business' while ensuring market safety and investor protection.
*   **Immediate Effect:** Yeh naye rules immediately effect mein aa gaye hain.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.sebi.gov.in/legal/circulars/mar-2026/review-of-coverage-of-settlement-guarantee-fund-for-commodity-derivatives-segment_85459.html)

*Published on: 17 March 2026*
